prompts: Dude, that was my message from a side fish. I don't want you to fix the reply watcher or the webhook poll I just wanna know why the fucking librarian wrote an amazing script for the pipeline for the orchestrator program, but it didn't fucking work. I don't me and another fish are doing the fish tank. And I told him to go and get the brain's trust opinion through the orchestrator, through the pipeline, because I need the Brains Trust to crunch through the brain. That's what I need you to fix.

Yeah. Can you fix it? And can you send out that last mission that they'd be in the the work fish did? We want them to go crunch the brain. And and make us a a document. Can you fire that one off?
───

"task_dir": "ORCHESTRATOR/complete_business_kb_compilation_20260320",        "plan": {            "task_id": "complete_business_kb_compilation_20260320",            "pattern": "pipeline",            "summary": "Compile complete business knowledge base covering all job types, rules, and workflows across three businesses",            "dispatches": [                {                    "to": "deepseek_fish",                    "message": "STAGE 1: Extract and compile ALL job types, service categories, and booking requirements from these files: /TOM/TOM_OPERATIONS_MANUAL.md, /BUSINESS/OVEN_REPAIRS_PRICING.md, /BUSINESS/ANTENNA_PRICING.md, /BUSINESS/SKY_SIGNAL_PRICING.md, /BUSINESS/SERVICE_AREAS.md, /BUSINESS/JOB_FLOW.md, /BUSINESS/PRICING_GUIDE.md, /TOM/KB_V2/kb-oven-deep.txt. Create a structured document covering: 1) All job types for each business (oven repairs, antenna work, electrical, Starlink, TV mounting, etc), 2) Required questions for each job type, 3) Booking requirements and restrictions, 4) Service area limitations (especially NORTH OF RIVER rule for electrical). Save to: /ORCHESTRATOR/complete_business_kb_compilation_20260320/stage1_job_types_and_booking.md",                    "priority": "normal",                    "stage": 1                },                {                    "to": "gpt_fish",                    "message": "STAGE 2: After deepseek completes stage 1, read their output and compile ALL business rules, restrictions, and operational procedures from: /TOM/TOM_GOLDEN_RULES.md, /CANONICAL/TOM_MASTER.txt, /BUSINESS/INSTANT_RECALL_FACTS.json, /BUSINESS/SHARON_WORKFLOW.md, /TOM/!_TOM_IS_SACRED_read_TOM_GOLDEN_RULES_before_touching_ANYTHING_one_change_sandbox_first_3_live_calls_before_next.md. Focus on: 1) Sacred business rules (north of river only, no CCTV, brand restrictions), 2) Tom's operational constraints and golden rules, 3) Workflow procedures for booking/scheduling, 4) Staff roles and responsibilities. Combine with stage 1 output. Save to: /ORCHESTRATOR/complete_business_kb_compilation_20260320/stage2_rules_and_workflows.md",                    "priority": "normal",

Dude, why would I want quick brain? Why would I want a dumb fucking answer, bro? The whole point of the fucking pipeline is to give us the best fucking answer, and you you're on about firing it to quick brain. Why the fuck would I want quick brain? The whole point of orchestrator is literally fire and fire it and forget. Come back in five or ten minutes, and you've got the best fucking answer in the world. What is the fuck is the point of quick brain? No. Bro, that's fucking dumb. Why would i want that?
